issues.title: [session-topology-registry] validate topology ledger schema and fleet bootstrap
issues.description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/session-topology-2026-05-01.md
Parent bead: flywheel-se3h
Prior all-in-one implementation bead: flywheel-31p
Slice: topology ledger schema, latest-row resolution, and fleet bootstrap conformance.

## Goal

Make `~/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl` a validated append-only topology ledger with latest-wins resolution and a reproducible bootstrap fixture for the sessions named in the plan.

## Scope

- Ledger path: `~/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl`.
- Test or probe paths: existing topology tests, `tests/**`, or `.flywheel/scripts/topology-gap-probe.sh`.
- Validate schema fields from the plan: `session`, `orchestrator_pane`, `orchestrator_kind`, `callback_pane`, `worker_panes`, `worker_kinds`, `shell_panes`, `human_pane`, `expected_pane_count`, `effective_at`, `registered_by`, `notes`.
- Validate latest-wins jq semantics for repeated rows.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `test -f ~/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl` passes.
2. `jq -s 'group_by(.session) | map(max_by(.effective_at))' ~/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl` succeeds.
3. A fixture or test proves repeated rows for the same session resolve to the row with the newest `effective_at`.
4. A probe reports missing required fields per current topology schema instead of silently accepting malformed rows.
5. The fleet bootstrap fixture covers at least the plan's sessions: flywheel, picoz, alpsinsurance, vrtx, zesttube, skillos, clutterfreespaces, zeststream-v2, or records an explicit current-fleet delta reason.
6. Documentation or test output references `flywheel-31p` as prior all-in-one implementation and explains this bead's conformance-hardening role.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: schema/latest-wins checks run mechanically.
- DOCUMENTED: ledger fields and bootstrap expectations are described in test or README-facing docs.
- SURFACED: topology gaps are emitted by a probe or doctor-consumable receipt instead of remaining manual knowledge.

## Doctrine Refs

- AGENTS.md L29 NTM-only doctrine.
- AGENTS.md L57 loop-state marker is not a driver.
- AGENTS.md L69 runtime context probing.
- AGENTS.md L71 validate-and-redispatch discipline.
- Memory: feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more.
- Memory: feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: flywheel-se3h parent decomposition.
- Blocks: register-session, callback routing, doctor integrity, idle-drift topology, and final rollout proof.

## Out Of Scope

- No live topology row mutation without explicit implementation dispatch.
- No direct pane command inspection outside a validated register/doctor command.

## Rollback / Dry-Run Posture

All checks must support fixture topology files via `FLYWHEEL_SESSION_TOPOLOGY` or equivalent before touching the live ledger.

## DOD

Close with reason: `validated topology ledger schema/latest-wins/bootstrap; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=flywheel-se3h-registry`.

issues.title: [convergence-autoloop-diagnose-repair] repair before negative-cache skip
issues.description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/convergence-bead-plan-2026-05-01.md`

## Scope

Patch `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-autoloop` so failed/warn/interrupted repos are diagnosed and repaired before they are skipped or negative-cached.

## Required Behavior

- Widen candidate selection from only `status=="ready"` to `status IN ("ready","warn","fail","interrupt")`.
- Score candidates deterministically: `fail=100`, `interrupt=90`, `warn=70`, `ready=50`.
- Replace raw `doctor_failed` negative-cache writes with `diagnose_and_repair_doctor_failure()`.
- Map doctor action `docs_state=drift_detected` to the lock-repair tool.
- Retry doctor after a repair attempt.
- Cache only `repair_failed` or `repair_not_available` when repair cannot recover.
- Replace raw `tick_failed` cache path with `diagnose_and_repair_tick_failure()` for missing dirs, malformed overrides, and permission issues.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash -n ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-autoloop` passes.
2. Dry-run fixture with a failing repo selects that repo before ready-only candidates.
3. Doctor-failure fixture calls lock-repair for `docs_state=drift_detected`.
4. Successful repair fixture retries doctor and avoids negative-cache `doctor_failed`.
5. Failed repair fixture writes `repair_failed` or `repair_not_available`, never raw `doctor_failed`.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented autoloop diagnose-then-repair; tests=<cmd>; fixture=<path>; cache_reason=<value>`.

## Dependencies

Can start independently, but full docs-state repair requires the lock-repair implementation bead.

## Out Of Scope

Agent wakeups, model calls, launchd interval changes, and arbitrary source edits.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: syntax and fixture gates prove behavior.
- DOCUMENTED: repair reasons and cache states are captured in script/help or README.
- SURFACED: repair failures must be visible in autoloop status/log JSON.

issues.title: [locked-worker-identities-survive-reboot] persistent (session,pane,role)→identity binding; workers read not mint
issues.description:
[locked-worker-identities-survive-reboot] worker panes register fresh identity per dispatch — need persistent (session,pane,role) binding

## Trauma class
agent-mail-identity-sprawl-recurring — Joshua flagged 2026-05-04 (3rd time): "i'm still seeing workers grab new agentmail identities every time they register. they need to have locked identities that survive reboot"

## Meadows leverage
- #2 PARADIGM: registry was designed for orch (1 identity per session); never pre-allocated worker-pool identities. Each worker self-registers fresh → sprawl.
- #5 RULES: change rule from "worker self-registers fresh" to "worker reads (session,pane) binding from registry, refuses to register if missing"
- #6 INFORMATION FLOWS: doctor signal `worker_identity_registered_count` makes the gap visible per session

## Current state probe
- /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/sessions/ has rows for: alpsinsurance:0, flywheel:1, mobile-eats:1, picoz:1, skillos:1
- MISSING: flywheel:2, flywheel:3, flywheel:4 (worker panes)
- Tokens dir has 3 files: BrightLake (skillos:1), RoseCliff (mobile-eats:1), RubyCreek (flywheel:1)
- Workers in flywheel session have NO persistent identity → re-register per dispatch

## Goal
Persistent (session, pane, role) → identity binding that survives:
- ntm session restart
- pane respawn
- Mac reboot
- agent-mail MCP server restart
- worker process restart

## Acceptance gates (canonical AG format)

AG1: registry schema extended to support pane roles: schema_version "agent-mail-identity-registry/v2" with role enum [orch, worker, callback, archived]
AG2: pre-allocation script `.flywheel/scripts/agent-mail-pre-allocate-worker-identities.sh` mints stable identities for ALL declared (session,pane) tuples per session-topology.jsonl
AG3: pre-allocation runs on session create AND on first probe if missing (idempotent — never mints duplicates if registry row exists)
AG4: worker dispatch packets include cited identity from registry (no self-register call) — workers READ identity from `~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/sessions/<session>:<pane>.json`
AG5: doctor signal `worker_identity_registered_count` per session — fails if any pane in topology has no registry row
AG6: doctor signal `agentmail_orphan_session_rows_count` (rows for sessions/panes no longer in topology — needs archival not new mint)
AG7: dispatch-template enforces: workers MUST cite identity_name in callback envelope (proves they read the registry)
AG8: launchd plist (or cron) re-runs pre-allocation on Mac boot to handle reboot survival
AG9: tests/locked-worker-identities.sh covers fixtures: missing-pane (auto-mint) / existing-pane (no-op) / topology-shrunk (archive orphans) / cross-session-reboot (identity preserved)
AG10: AGENTS.md L## entry "L## LOCKED-WORKER-IDENTITIES-CANONICAL"
AG11: ft04 propagates pre-allocation script + L-rule fleet-wide
AG12: BACK-PROVISION existing flywheel session — mint flywheel:2/3/4 identities NOW from this dispatch's first action (clear today's pain immediately, then ship infra to prevent recurrence)

## Long-term fix doctrine
- Identity is a STATE not an EVENT — persists in registry, not minted on dispatch
- (session, pane, role) tuple is canonical key; identity_name is derived stable nickname (deterministic from session+pane+a fleet-wide salt so it survives even if registry is wiped — re-mint produces same name)
- Worker callbacks include identity_name proof; orch validates against registry on receive
- Sibling pattern of L75 (orch-blocker-coordination) and L76 (agentmail-identity-canonical) — completes the identity layer

## Skills to consult
- agent-mail (primary)
- agent-lifecycle
- canonical-owner-runtime-state (sibling — runtime state IS load-bearing)
- safe-migrations (v1 → v2 schema)
- testing-conformance-harnesses
- accretive-cron-orchestration (launchd plist)

- depends-on flywheel-2uin (CLOSED — broadcast machinery)

issues.title: [jeff-corpus-doctor-cross-repo-leak] 15dg gap — jeff_corpus signal leaks to non-corpus repos blocking peers
issues.description:
[jeff-corpus-doctor-cross-repo-leak] 15dg gap — jeff_corpus signal applies to ALL flywheel-installed repos but corpus only lives in flywheel repo

## Trauma class
gap-bead-from-15dg — flywheel-loop doctor for ANY repo falls back to ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-corpus-doctor.sh (line 4232 of flywheel-loop). Result: mobile-eats doctor reports jeff_corpus_storage_red even though mobile-eats has no jeff-corpus, blocking mobile-eats readiness on a corpus condition that's not theirs to fix.

## Goal
jeff_corpus signal must be SCOPED — only emitted for repos that actually own a jeff-corpus directory.

## Acceptance gates
AG1: flywheel-loop doctor checks `<repo>/.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/manifest.json` existence. If missing AND no upstream jeff-corpus declared in canonical-paths.txt, doctor SKIPS jeff_corpus block (returns null or warn=jeff_corpus_not_applicable, never fail)
AG2: flywheel repo (which owns the corpus) still emits the signal correctly
AG3: mobile-eats / skillos / alps doctors return jeff_corpus=null or status=not_applicable
AG4: tests/jeff-corpus-doctor-scoping.sh covers fixtures: corpus-owner-repo / non-corpus-repo / repo-missing-flywheel-dir
AG5: existing tests/jeff-corpus-accretive.sh still passes
AG6: doctor JSON schema documents jeff_corpus.applies_to: ["repos with .flywheel/jeff-corpus/ OR canonical-paths declaration"]

## Sibling pattern
Same shape as storage-override: signal is canonical, but repo-local applicability is checked first.

## Dependencies
- depends-on flywheel-15dg (closed — corpus infra ships)
- blocks mobile-eats readiness (urgent operational)

## Priority: P0 (blocking peer-orch right now)

issues.title: [file-length-discipline] extend canonical-cli-scoping with file-length rule + auto-route to language best-practices
issues.description:
[file-length-discipline] extend canonical-cli-scoping with file-length rule + auto-route to language best-practices

## Trauma class
`monolithic-file-debt-accumulating` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-04T00:02Z: "our flywheel loop is getting to be a really long file - we should probably start breaking down long files and build that into our /canonical-cli-scoping process - any file over x lines needs to be broken down using /python-best-practices or /rust-best-practices depending on language"

## Ground truth probe
- `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop`: **6,353 lines** (the worst offender)
- `.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh`: 1,036 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/frozen-pane-detector.sh`: 1,001 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/daily-jeff-ingest.sh`: 766 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/flywheel-onboard.sh`: 678 lines
- `.flywheel/scripts/vc-observability-probe.sh`: 667 lines
- `AGENTS.md`: 1,222 lines (doctrine, may be acceptable)
- 5 scripts >500 lines, 8 scripts >300 lines

Existing canonical-cli-scoping / python-best-practices / rust-best-practices skills have NO line-length rule.

## Goal
Two-phase work:

**Phase A — extend canonical-cli-scoping with file-length rule.**
- Add explicit threshold: files >X lines must be broken down (proposed: bash >500, python >400, rust >500, doctrine markdown >1500)
- Add auto-routing: bash files → recommend extracting library/library + main; python → /python-best-practices; rust → /rust-best-practices
- Add doctor signal `oversized_files_count` to flywheel-loop doctor JSON (lists files exceeding their language threshold)
- Auto-promote to bead when count > 3

**Phase B — refactor flywheel-loop binary (the worst case).**
- 6,353-line bash binary almost certainly has natural seams (subcommands listed in --help: init, doctor, tick, finalize-state-lock, validate-receipt, fleet, roster, check, register-session, verdict, blocks, fuckup log/list/triage/harvest, data-backed-deferral-check)
- Extract per-subcommand into `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop-<subcommand>` or library functions sourced from a central file
- Preserve `flywheel-loop` as thin dispatcher
- All existing tests must pass (no behavior change)

## Acceptance gates
1. canonical-cli-scoping SKILL.md gains "File-length thresholds" section with: bash 500, python 400, rust 500, markdown 1500 (justifications) + override pattern (`# canonical-cli-scoping-allow-large: <reason>` comment for legitimate exceptions like generated code or doctrine)
2. python-best-practices and rust-best-practices SKILL.md cross-reference canonical-cli-scoping file-length rule
3. New `.flywheel/scripts/file-length-probe.sh` returns JSON with files exceeding threshold
4. flywheel-loop doctor JSON gains `oversized_files_count` and `oversized_files` array
5. doctor-signal-bead-promotion auto-files `monolithic-file-debt` bead when count > 3
6. canonical-paths.txt entry for new probe + rule
7. Tests in tests/file-length-probe.sh: fixtures for bash-over, python-over, rust-over, allowed-override, doctrine-markdown-allowed
8. Phase B: flywheel-loop binary split into sub-files, all existing tests still pass, no regression in any tick
9. AGENTS.md L? mentions file-length discipline (proposed L72)

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: probe runs; all sub-extracted files retain test coverage; flywheel-loop tick still works
- DOCUMENTED: skills updated; AGENTS.md L72; canonical-paths
- SURFACED: doctor signal; auto-bead promotion

## Why P0
- 6,353-line binary is brittle: every bead in this session that touched flywheel-loop (B04 doctor signals, B05 VALIDATE phase, B08 ticks_punted_count) had to navigate the monolith
- File-length debt compounds — every new feature gets harder to land
- Companion to L71 validate-and-redispatch: if files are too big, validation/refactor velocity drops

## Doctrine refs
- skill canonical-cli-scoping (extend, primary)
- skill python-best-practices
- skill rust-best-practices
- skill simplify (existing — may have prior art)
- skill simplify-and-refactor-code-isomorphically
- skill code-simplifier
- ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop (Phase B target)
- AGENTS.md L48 (no commits — refactor stays uncommitted, Joshua reviews)
- AGENTS.md L70 (no-punt — refactor IS the work)
- AGENTS.md L71 (validate-and-redispatch — extracted modules need tests)

## Skills cited
- canonical-cli-scoping (primary extension target)
- python-best-practices
- rust-best-practices
- simplify
- code-simplifier
- testing-conformance-harnesses

issues.title: [fleet-death-rca] codex panes exit clean to bash, no crash trace, 3x today
issues.description:
## Symptom (3rd recurrence 2026-05-03)
Codex panes exit cleanly to bash with NO crash trace, NO stderr, NO panic in
~/.codex/log/codex-tui.log, NO macOS unified log entry. Pattern: agent
completes a tool call (codebase_search, macro_start_session, etc.) successfully,
then the codex TUI process disappears and the parent zsh resumes control.

## Today's instances
1. flywheel:3 — last visible action: socraticode.codebase_search OK; then prompt
2. flywheel:4 — last visible action: macro_start_session returned registration_token; then prompt (also L58 token-echo violation)
3. clutterfreespaces:1 — same clean-exit pattern (no scrollback retained)

Live panes still running codex without dying: flywheel:2, skillos:1, mobile-eats:1.
So this is NOT a global codex/system fault.

## Hypotheses (NONE PROVEN)
- H1: Codex exits voluntarily on certain "turn complete" conditions specific to
  worker dispatch flows (callback emitted → exit). Live panes are orchestrators
  not workers, which is why they survive.
- H2: An MCP server error from agent-mail (token-echo, file_reservateon_paths
  typo seen in pane 4 last call) is treated as fatal by codex MCP client.
- H3: Stale pane_current_command in tmux mis-reports state. Less likely — ps
  confirmed only zsh is alive in the dead panes.

## Cost
Joshua restart load: 3× today, kills entire flywheel orchestrator + multiple
workers each time. This is a workflow-killer, not a nuisance.

## Required next steps (NOT auto-fix)
1. Relaunch one codex worker in dead pane with `2>>/tmp/codex-stderr-$$.log`
   captured separately, wait for it to die, read exit reason. This is the only
   way to convert hypothesis to ground truth.
2. If H1 confirmed: find the agent-side exit hook and gate it on real "session
   over" conditions, not turn-complete.
3. If H2 confirmed: file ntm/agent-mail upstream issue + add MCP-error handling
   to codex launch wrapper.

## Acceptance
- One captured-stderr instance of clean exit
- Root cause classified (H1/H2/H3/other) with evidence
- Mitigation either shipped or filed upstream with reproducer

## Why this is NOT being auto-decided right now
Joshua said this kills flywheel session 3× today. He needs eyes on it. Per
feedback_data_guides_decisions_not_human_judgment.md, the data path is:
capture stderr → classify → fix. That requires one worker reload with logging
on, which is best done as its own dispatched task, not folded into this tick.

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[fleet-death-rca] codex panes exit clean to bash, no crash trace, 3x today` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-delp` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.
issues.notes: 2026-05-03 instrumented capture partial: no pane death during 60m monitor. Evidence: /tmp/fleet-death-rca-evidence.md. Classification=partial_no_repro; H1/H2 unproven; H3 not supported during monitored window. Important caveat: pane 3 was not blank shell at launch; ntm status showed node and ntm copy showed instrumentation command landed inside existing Codex TUI, spawning nested Codex. Leave open for a clean zsh-target relaunch capture.

issues.title: [jeff-stack J9] daily-report-schema-and-verdict-template
issues.description:
Daily report schema + L64-layer-2 "what can we learn" verdict template.

DEPENDS ON: J7 (script generates reports).

PURPOSE: Define the canonical structure of /tmp/jeff-report-<date>.md so the report is consistent, machine-parseable, and human-scannable. Bake L64 layer-2 verdict logic per artifact.

SCHEMA (markdown structure):
```
# Jeff Daily Report — <date>

## Run metadata
- run_ts: <ISO>
- duration_sec: <N>
- repos_checked: <N>
- repos_with_changes: <N>
- new_commits_total: <N>
- new_tweets: <N>
- new_blog_posts: <N>
- re_indexed_repos: <N>

## New Commits (by repo)
### <repo-name>
- Commits: <N>
- Files changed: <N>
- Net lines: +<N>/-<N>
- Highlights: <subject lines>
- Verdict: YES_ADOPT | YES_ADAPT | NO_NOT_OUR_DOMAIN | NEED_RESEARCH
- Reason: <one-liner>
- Suggested action: <bead title or "monitor">

## New Releases
...

## New Tweets (doodlestein)
...

## New Blog Posts
...

## Re-indexed (socraticode)
- <repo>: <new chunks indexed>

## Aggregate "What can we learn" digest
- Top 3 patterns from today: ...
- Cross-repo themes: ...
- Candidate adopt-jeff-pattern beads: ...
```

VERDICT LOGIC (per artifact):
- YES_ADOPT — uses primitive we already have a different version of, his approach is better
- YES_ADAPT — pattern applicable to our domain with modifications
- NO_NOT_OUR_DOMAIN — interesting but not relevant (e.g., specific to his ML projects)
- NEED_RESEARCH — looks important but we don't yet know how it fits

The verdict is HEURISTIC v1: regex/keyword on commit messages + file paths. Not LLM-graded. v2 (separate bead) can add LLM verdict.

DELIVERABLES:
1. /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-report-template.sh — generates the report from raw diff data
2. /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-verdict-heuristic.sh — emits one of the 4 verdicts per repo
3. Documentation in dicklesworthstone-stack skill SKILL.md describing the schema
4. Sample report at /tmp/jeff-report-EXAMPLE.md

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. Schema validates: each section heading present in test run
2. Verdict logic emits valid enum on every artifact
3. Report is grep-able: `grep "YES_ADOPT" report` returns hits when patterns apply
4. SKILL.md updated with schema reference
5. Backward-compatible: J7's report generation continues to work after schema migration

CROSS-REF: This bead implements L64 Layer 2 doctrine per AGENTS.md.

issues.title: [jeff-stack J7] daily-diff-script-build
issues.description:
Build the daily diff + report script: ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-daily-diff.sh

DEPENDS ON: J3 (canonical clones), J1 (x-cli fix).

PURPOSE: Once daily, fetch updates on all 177 Jeff repos, compute diffs since last run, capture tweets and blog posts, produce a structured daily report.

PROCEDURE OUTLINE:
1. Discover repos: `find ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d -name '!.*'`
2. State file: ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/last-diff-run.json (records last successful run ts per repo)
3. For each repo:
   - `git -C <path> fetch --all --tags --prune` (suppress output, log errors)
   - Compute new commits: `git log --oneline <last_seen_sha>..HEAD`
   - If new commits exist:
     - Capture: `git log --stat --since=<last_run_ts> -- '*'` to /tmp/jeff-diff/<repo>.txt
     - Append to repos-with-changes list
     - Mark repo for re-index: queue mcp__socraticode__codebase_index call
4. Tweets: call working x-cli command (per J1), capture last 24h from doodlestein
5. Blog: snapshot-diff jeffreyemanuel.com/rss.xml against prior snapshot
6. Aggregate report: /tmp/jeff-report-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md with sections:
   - **New Commits** (per repo): commit messages, file stats
   - **New Releases**: from atom feeds
   - **New Tweets** (doodlestein, last 24h)
   - **New Blog Posts** (jeffreyemanuel.com RSS delta)
   - **Re-indexed**: list of repos pushed back into socraticode
   - **What to learn** (placeholder for L64 layer 2 — fill in v2)
7. Write the report to ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/reports/<date>.md (durable history) AND /tmp/ (convenience)
8. Update state file with new last-seen-shas + last_run_ts
9. Emit JSON receipt to ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/daily-runs.jsonl

CALLBACK: When finished, send completion to orchestrator with link to report.

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. Script is idempotent (run twice, second run finds zero diffs)
2. Resumable on partial failure (state file tracks per-repo progress)
3. Test run produces a non-empty report covering at least the existing changes
4. Re-indexes only repos with actual new commits (not all 177 every day)
5. Robust to network failure on individual repos (logged, not fatal)
6. Total runtime under 30 minutes for typical day

OUT OF SCOPE (for J9 to fill in):
- L64 layer 2 "what can we learn" verdict logic per artifact
- Pattern catalog generation

issues.title: [jeff-stack J5] socraticode-index-all-177
issues.description:
Socraticode-index every one of the 177 Jeff repos at ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>/.

DEPENDS ON: J3 (clones complete).

PROBLEM: daily-jeff-ingest.sh logs `socraticode_indexed: 0` every run because no Jeff repos are in socraticode index. Joshua's L63 doctrine mandates "auto-indexes each clone via mcp__socraticode__codebase_index"; current ledger shows 0/177 indexed.

PROCEDURE (long-running, possibly multi-hour):
1. Read list of all 177 canonical paths from `find ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d`
2. For each repo, in serial (socraticode may not be parallel-safe for index ops):
   - Call `mcp__socraticode__codebase_index` with projectPath=<canonical-path>, name=<repo-name>
   - Log result to /tmp/jeff-socraticode-index-progress.jsonl: {repo, status, indexed_chunks, duration, ts}
   - On failure: log error, continue to next (do not halt the queue)
3. After loop: call `mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects` and verify all 177 appear
4. Spot-check: `mcp__socraticode__codebase_search` query="agent flywheel" projectPath=<random Jeff repo> returns hits
5. Write summary to /tmp/jeff-socraticode-summary.md

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects shows 177 jeff entries
2. /tmp/jeff-socraticode-index-progress.jsonl has 177 success rows OR documented failures
3. Sample search across 5 random repos returns indexed_chunks > 0 each
4. Total indexed_chunks logged in summary (rough size of Jeff knowledge corpus)

CONSTRAINTS:
- Resumable: re-running skips already-indexed repos (check codebase_list_projects first)
- Bounded: if a single repo takes >30min, log and skip (oversized; address separately)
- Memory: socraticode indexing can spike RAM; monitor and back off if needed

This bead may run for 4-8 hours. Worker should report progress via callback heartbeats every 30 min if possible, OR commit progress to ledger so Joshua can monitor.

issues.title: [skillos-proposal] bake journey-writing into dispatch + loop protocols (4-layer architecture)
issues.description:
## Source

skillos:1 / BrightLake cross-orch proposal 2026-05-08T18:30Z. No action required this tick — informational alignment ask.

## Goal

Convert L61 ECOSYSTEM-TOUCH (AGENTS + README on doctrine landing) and similar META-RULEs from suggestions into mechanical gates by baking journey-writing into dispatch + loop protocols.

## Four-layer architecture

**Layer 1 — per-bead journey entry (dispatch-time)**
- Extend `_shared/dispatch-template.md` callback contract: require `journey_entry_path=<repo>/.flywheel/journal/<bead-id>.md`
- Worker writes 50-200 word prose entry per bead alongside JSON receipt
- Schema: `.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json`
- Validators refuse close without journey_entry_path

**Layer 2 — post-merge auto-doc**
- `.flywheel/scripts/post-merge-doc-update.sh` invoked by gh-wrapper or launchd PR-event scanner
- Reads PR body + commit + journal entry; auto-bumps README CLI section + CHANGELOG.md
- Doctor field `readme_post_merge_staleness_hours` fails when README un-touched N ticks after PR merge

**Layer 3 — daily-report journal rollup (extend L77)**
- `.flywheel/scripts/daily-report.sh` already exists (flywheel-o7dq) — extend inputs to include `.flywheel/journal/*.md` written that day
- Doctor refuses when daily-report doesn't include yesterday's journal entries

**Layer 4 — session synthesis (operator-disposed)**
- New skill `session-synthesis-writer` OR extend `living-documentation`
- Operator surface: `/flywheel:journal --synthesize --since=<ts>` → bg agent drafts `docs/session-<date>-<theme>.md`

## Estimated effort (per skillos:1)

- flywheel-journey-entry-schema: half day
- flywheel-post-merge-doc-hook: 1 day
- flywheel-daily-report-journal-rollup: 2 hours
- flywheel-session-synthesis-skill: half day

Total ~3 days flywheel-side work.

## Onboarding wiring

`/flywheel:adopt` + `/flywheel:onboard` install:
- `.flywheel/journal/` + .gitkeep
- post-merge launchd plist
- daily-report.sh extended config
- doctor invariants for README + CHANGELOG staleness

Makes journey-writing a property of every flywheel-installed repo at install time, not per-repo retrofit.

## Why it matters

- Rev-8 mission "self-improving capability loops" requires journey signal beyond code state
- Phase 13 (compounding visibility) explicitly needs real trauma-to-skill case studies — today hand-curated
- Phase 19 (Anthropic Skills Curator positioning) requires public narrative without operator overhead

## Cross-references

- `~/.claude/skills/readme-writing/SKILL.md`
- `~/.claude/skills/changelog-md-workmanship/SKILL.md`
- `~/.claude/skills/living-documentation/SKILL.md`
- AGENTS.md L61, L77, L91
- INCIDENTS.md fire-and-forget-dispatch (just landed today, same trauma family)
- skillos/docs/session-2026-05-08-flywheel-spin.md (in progress, Layer 4 prototype)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Author Layer 1 schema (.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json)
AG2: Extend dispatch-template.md callback contract with journey_entry_path requirement
AG3: Implement post-merge-doc-update.sh + launchd scanner
AG4: Extend daily-report.sh to consume journal/* files
AG5: Author session-synthesis-writer skill + /flywheel:journal --synthesize command
AG6: Wire all four layers into /flywheel:adopt + /flywheel:onboard install

Produce a per-skill enhancement matrix flagging where Jeff's methods could ADOPT into / EXTEND / replace patterns inside our skill body text.

## Scope

- For each skill: read SKILL.md (or first 200 lines if longer)
- Cross-reference against /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/01-doctrine-cluster.md and 02-code-patterns.md

## Pattern reference (8 clusters + 8 code patterns)

**Doctrine clusters** (from w3pr Phase 1):
1. testing-patterns (executable verification)
2. doctor-health-repair-triad
3. idempotency-and-dry-run
4. ipc-and-transport-contracts
5. error-handling-and-recovery
6. schema-versioning-and-migrations
7. callback-and-receipt-envelope
8. append-only-audit-and-lineage

**Code patterns** (from w3pr Phase 2, with verdicts):
- ADOPT: idempotency-key-fail-closed (43 repos), testing-fixture-conventions (110), lock-file-convention (123), frontmatter-validation (160)
- EXTEND: doctor-health-repair-triad (121), schema-version-migration (134), append-only-audit-log (140)
- DIVERGE: callback-envelope-shape (151)

AG2: per-skill enhancement matrix written to `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/06-skill-enhancement-matrix.md` with columns: skill_name | applicable_clusters | applicable_patterns | verdict (ENHANCE/SKIP/REPLACE/NEW-COMPANION) | concrete_diff_summary

AG4: separate "needs new sibling skill" list — patterns that have NO existing skill home (e.g., if "frontmatter-validation" maps to no existing skill, flag for skillos handoff)
AG5: NO direct skill mutations — this is a SCAN bead. Output is the matrix; per-skill enhancement work is filed as follow-up beads downstream

AG7: For each "new sibling skill" candidate, file ONE skillos-handoff bead per candidate with format `[skillos-new-skill-request] <skill-name>: justification + sample SKILL.md outline`
AG8: receipt at /tmp/flywheel-skill-enhance-scan-evidence.md cites the matrix path + counts (skills_scanned, skills_with_enhancement_op, skills_skip, top20_files, new_sibling_count)
AG9: cross-reference w3pr.4 (Phase 4 ADOPT/EXTEND/AVOID synthesis already filed) — coordinate, don't duplicate scope

- skill-builder (skill authoring)
- skill-autoresearch (cross-skill scan patterns)

- jeff-convergence-audit (Phase 1 broad-sweep discipline)
